mappings.rationaleAI: Modern China addresses the Western challenge (A.1), westernization versus tradition (A.2), and revolutionary movements (A.2.d).
Modern Japan addresses the Meiji Restoration (B.1.a), modernization (B.1.b), and Meiji politics (B.1.c).
Imperial expansion connects to Western penetration (A.1) and foreign encroachments (A.2.b).
Nationalism connects to Chinese revolutionary movements (A.2.d) and Japanese national loyalties (B.1.b).
Revolutionary theory connects to reformist movements (A.2.d) and the 1911 Revolution (A.2.d.ii).
Confucianism's relationship with Western ideas connects to westernization tensions (A.2).
Samurai dissolution connects to the Tokugawa fall (B.1.a).
Korea connects to the Sino-Japanese War (A.2.b).
Industrialization connects to Chinese self-strengthening (A.2.a.ii) and Japanese modernization (B.1.b).
Capitalist systems connect to Japanese zaibatsu (B.1.b).
Democracy connects to Chinese constitutional movements (A.2.d.i) and Meiji institutions (B.1.c).
Global integration connects to the collapse of the tributary system (A.1).
Diplomatic relations connect to Chinese diplomatic hesitancy (A.2.b).
Economic development connects to China's challenges (A.1) and Japan's modernization (B.1.b).
Sun Yat-sen's United League and the 1911 Revolution provide essential historical context for understanding Mao's later rise (A.2.d).
